title: 5 Reporting CEBA Loan Administration Fees
body_text:
5	Reporting CEBA Loan Administration Fees
5.1	Overview of Reporting CEBA Loan Administration Fees
Your financial institution must report the loan administration fee to Central 1 for CEBA loan applications. You must report the fee at the end of each calculation period before the 15th day of the next calculation period.
In general, the fiscal quarterly calculation periods are Q1 ending January 31, Q2 ending April 30, Q3 ending July 31, and Q4 ending October 31. See the Calculation Period List provided with the CEBA Loan Administration Fee Report for specific dates. You can also refer to your financial institutionâs Credit Agreement for more information.
5.2	Completing the CEBA Program â Loan Administration Fee Report in ServiceNow

3.1

Figure 1: Selecting the Loan Administration Fee Report
Your financial institutionâs name and the reporting period are auto-populated. Complete the form for loan administration fee reporting as shown in Figure 2:

â Enter the amount you calculated for your financial institutionâs quarterly loan administration fee.
â£
Loan Administration Fee Due for Quarter
â This field displays the amount payable by EDC. If your calculated fee is less than $2,500, EDC will increase the threshold to $2,500 (e.g., if your fee is $1,800, EDC will pay $2,500). If your calculated fee is $2,500 or more, EDC will pay the amount of the loan administration fee (e.g., if your fee is $3,000, EDC will pay $3,000).
